summary: The main claim is: Security risks -------------- If you're 90% through your DWIM AI development and it seems very promising, then you must be very careful with your code and data.
You should be careful with your team members too since threats and blackmail and bribes are all quite cheap and effective.

Infra-Bayesian logic is not the only way to define an infra-Bayesian analog of AIXI.

As the AGI forms its values, they will be derived from the abstractions in its world model.

The main claim is: We have a lot of experience and knowledge of building systems that are broadly beneficial and safe, while operating in the human capabilities regime.
Exploit the Human Regime We want systems that are as safe as humans, for the same reasons that humans have (or don’t have) those safety properties.

The main claim is: If we want to be extra careful, we can never act on what the system outputs during training, so there should be no reason for it to learn to manipulate humans.
